ai_research.headline: OpenClaw v2026.4.14 drops GPT-5.4 support and Active Memory plugin — Tesla tapes out AI5 chip

items.title: OpenClaw v2026.4.14 — GPT-5.4 + Telegram improvements
items.body: Released April 14. Adds GPT-5.4 support (including pro variant pricing/limits), improved Telegram forum topic handling, Ollama stability fixes, media/vision fixes, and SSRF security hardening. No breaking changes — update via: npm install -g openclaw@latest

items.title: OpenClaw v2026.4.12 — Active Memory plugin lands
items.body: Released April 13. Introduces the Active Memory plugin — a dedicated sub-agent for context recall. Also adds LM Studio and Codex provider support, local macOS speech, and an exec-policy CLI. Big one for agent builders who want persistent memory without managing it themselves.

items.title: xAI sued by NAACP over Memphis data center pollution
items.body: NAACP filed suit against xAI alleging Clean Air Act violations from 27 unpermitted natural gas turbines powering Colossus data centers in Memphis and Southaven, MS. Separately, xAI is suing Colorado over a new AI consumer protection law, arguing it violates free speech.

ai_research.learn_today: Read the OpenClaw Active Memory plugin docs — persistent context recall without manual memory management is exactly what makes agents feel less stateless.

openclaw_agents.headline: Active Memory sub-agent is the week's most useful new agent primitive

items.title: Active Memory plugin — dedicated recall sub-agent
items.body: v2026.4.12 ships a sub-agent specifically for context recall. Instead of hoping the model remembers things, you now have a dedicated agent that stores and retrieves relevant context on demand. This is the right architecture for long-running agents.
items.try_this: Update to v2026.4.12+ and enable the Active Memory plugin. Test it with a task that spans multiple sessions.

items.title: LM Studio + Codex provider support added
items.body: You can now point OpenClaw at LM Studio for local model inference, or use Codex as a provider. More options for running models locally without API costs.
items.try_this: If you have LM Studio set up, try connecting it to OpenClaw and running a task locally — useful for offline or cost-sensitive work.

items.title: Exec-policy CLI — control what agents can run
items.body: New exec-policy CLI lets you define what shell commands agents are allowed to execute. Useful for locking down agents that run on shared or sensitive machines.

openclaw_agents.try_today: Run: npm install -g openclaw@latest — you're on 2026.4.2, current is 2026.4.14. That's 12 versions behind.

tesla.note: AI5 chip taped out — design finalized, volume production targeted for 2027. AI6, Dojo3 also in development. Analyst upgrades following the news. Q1 earnings April 22. Stock up slightly on the chip news.

tesla_spacex_xai.spacex: Falcon 9 launched 25 Starlink satellites from Vandenberg last night. SpaceX IPO rumors heating up — potential $75B raise at $1.75T valuation later in 2026. Booster 19 static fire at Starbase.
tesla_spacex_xai.xai: NAACP lawsuit over Memphis data center gas turbines — real legal and PR risk. Also suing Colorado over AI consumer protection law. Grok deepfake issues still in news cycle. Colossus expansion continues despite the heat.

items.title: The Boys — Season 5
items.note: New episode dropped yesterday. If you're watching, you know. If you're not — it's the best superhero satire on TV and it's in its final season.

items.title: Invincible — Season 4
items.note: Also dropped a new episode. Animated superhero series that takes the genre seriously. Genuinely great if you haven't started.

items.title: Molly's Game
items.note: Aaron Sorkin wrote and directed. Jessica Chastain running an underground poker empire. Smart, fast, worth your time if you missed it.

items.title: Fake Profile — Season 3
items.note: Colombian telenovela. Unless that's your thing.

items.title: OpenClaw competitor landscape is shifting fast
items.body: RunLobster, SimpleClaw, Kimi Claw all active. The framing that's winning: 'a real computer, not a chatbot.' If InstaClaw isn't using that angle, it's leaving positioning on the table. The market is buying the productivity/employee narrative more than the feature list.

items.title: QuizzyDots r/webgames post — check for comments
items.body: Posted April 9 to r/WebGames. Monitor should be checking every 15 minutes. If comments came in, now's the time to respond — engagement within the first few hours matters most for Reddit ranking.

macro.mood: Markets quiet and positive. TSLA up on AI5 chip news. LMND +3.4% — momentum continuing. BTC holding $73K.
